name: Teicoplanin
executiveSummary: Treatment of lower respiratory tract, bone, skin and soft tissue, and urinary tract infections caused by gram-positive microorganisms susceptible to vancomycin when venous access is lost, as it can be administered by IM route.
indications:
## Main indications

Treatment of lower respiratory tract, bone, skin and soft tissue, and urinary tract infections caused by gram-positive microorganisms susceptible to vancomycin when venous access is lost, as it can be administered by IM route.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.reconstitution: Reconstitute with sterile water for injection; foaming is normal, allow to settle.
adulto.dose: Loading: 6 mg/kg every 12 h x 3 doses. Maintenance: 6 mg/kg every 24 h.

pediatrico.dose: Newborns: Day 1: 16 mg/kg/day, then 8 mg/kg/day (by infusion); > 2 months mild infections, first 3 doses IV/IM: 10 mg/kg/dose every 12 h, then: 10 mg/kg/day every 24 h; maximum dose: 400 mg/day. Neutropenic Bone Marrow Transplant severe infections: 15 - 20 mg/kg/day, maintain trough concentration: > 10 mg/L. Adults: 400 mg every 12 h for 3 doses, then mild infections: 400 mg every 24 h, severe infections: maximum dose 800 mg every 24 h.
pediatrico.notes: Its prolonged half-life allows use every 24 h. IV infusion over 30 minutes. Dose adjustment in renal failure.

adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Injection-site pain, fever, nausea, vomiting, anaphylactic reaction. Rare: thrombocytopenia.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — Teicoplanin (code 521, ATC J01XA).
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
Argentine Society of Infectious Diseases (SADI). Guidelines and consensus statements.
Infectious Diseases Society of America (IDSA). Clinical practice guidelines.
